extracted_json.title: 1.5 Product/Market Strategy (P/MS)

items.text: What differentiates the company's products from its competitors
Example (CISCO): reliability; security; Quality-of-service to its customers
What markets does the company serve?
Example (CISCO): Cisco segments its market into:

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Product/Market Strategy (P/MS): differentiation and market segmentation, illustrated with Cisco.
extracted_json.summary_gist: Notes on Product/Market Strategy organized as a two-column table (Definitions vs. Example using Cisco). Two key questions: (1) What differentiates the company's products from competitors — for Cisco, reliability, security, and quality-of-service to customers. (2) What markets does the company serve — Cisco segments its market into enterprises (Fortune 100), Internet Service Providers (AT&T, Comcast), Commercial (all other companies), and Consumers.

transcription:
1.5  Product/Market Strategy (P/MS)

Definitions          Example
                     (CISCO)

1. What differentiates    - reliability
   the company's products  - security
   from its competitors    - Quality-of-service
                             to its customers

2. What markets         Cisco segments
   does the company     its market into
   serve?               - enterprises: Fortune
                                     100 companies
                        - Internet Service Providers
                          (ISP): AT&T, Comcast, ....
                        - Commercial (all other
                            companies)
                        - Consumers
